note: The brochure uses INT343 for two different minor-specialization courses. This seed normalizes the e-learning course with a -EL suffix to keep course codes unique.
The brochure uses INT343 for two different minor-specialization courses. This seed normalizes the web-development course with a -WDT suffix to keep course codes unique.
The IDSS semester plan prints ISD442 for Database System (2), but the dedicated course table uses ISD342 for the same title and prerequisite. The seed keeps ISD342.
The brochure prints ISD4223 for Selected Topic in ISD. The seed normalizes this to ISD423.
The brochure prints ISD4215 for ISD Project (2). The seed normalizes this to ISD449 to match the ISD448 / ISD449 project sequence.
Sinai History is printed with total credit hours = 0 in the brochure, and the seed preserves that value.
The brochure does not include a standalone IDSS specialization requirements table. IDSS course categories and graduation-category totals were inferred from the published semester plan.
Different brochure tables list different prerequisites for Project Management. The seed keeps both ISD100 and ISD220.
Different brochure tables list different prerequisites for Network Optimization. The seed keeps both ISD220 and INT232.

The printed software-engineering table creates an impossible same-semester cycle among CSW463, CSW465, and CSW468. The seed normalizes CSW463 to depend on CSW464 so the sequence remains acyclic and consistent with the semester plan.

The printed software-engineering table places CSW464 after CSW463 in a way that conflicts with the semester plan. The seed normalizes CSW464 to depend on CSW362 so the sequence remains acyclic and semester-consistent.
